note: seq=47 (QUEUE-THROUGHPUT PROBE, READ-ONLY). Confirms InterServer is contributing to the SHARED atlas.enrich_queue and establishes the combined PG-load baseline for the ceiling test. Runs /opt/atlas/queue_throughput_probe.py which: samples count(status='done') twice over ~5s -> COMBINED done-rate/min (both nodes mark done in the same table); reports the per-claimant locked_by split (Hetzner pool-N vs InterServer) and the pg_stat_activity client_addr split (InterServer connects from 64.20.50.3; local Hetzner workers via unix socket/127.0.0.1) -> proves InterServer claiming + shows its PG connection count; reports pg_total_connections vs max_connections (the real combined constraint) and Hetzner load. NO writes. Publishes status/hetzner/queue-throughput.json. HONEST: not run against live PG from sandbox; py_compile clean + --selftest PASS (rate math + claimant bucketing). Allowlist-safe (/opt/atlas/).

steps.note: seq=47: read-only shared-queue throughput + claimant + pg-connection probe.
